description: Batch transaction command — atomically bundle multiple transactions with --build-only flag for composable shell substitution

userStories.title: --build-only flag on all commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want every command to support --build-only so I can generate unsigned transaction JSON for use in batch submissions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add --build-only boolean flag to all transaction commands: payment, trust, offer, channel, escrow, check, clawback, credential, nft, multisig, oracle, ticket, deposit-preauth, account set, account set-regular-key, account delete, did, mptoken issuance create/destroy/set, mptoken authorize, vault create/set/deposit/withdraw/delete/clawback, permissioned-domain create/update/delete
When --build-only is set: derive Account from key material without connecting to network; output JSON of tx fields + Account to stdout; exit 0
--build-only output does NOT include Fee, Sequence, LastLedgerSequence, SigningPubKey, TxnSignature
--build-only is incompatible with --dry-run, --no-wait, --json — CLI exits with error if combined
Key material (--seed/--mnemonic/--account+--keystore) is still required with --build-only

tests/e2e/batch/batch.build-only.test.ts: no network, no beforeAll; payment --build-only outputs valid JSON with TransactionType and Account; output has no Fee or Sequence; --build-only with --dry-run exits with error; --build-only with --no-wait exits with error; use hardcoded static seed and address values
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: --build-only is a pure offline operation — it only needs the key material to derive Account. No network connection required. This makes it safe to test without any faucet calls.

userStories.title: xrpl batch command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to submit multiple transactions atomically so I can guarantee they all succeed or handle failures according to a defined policy.
acceptanceCriteria: Read Batch transaction source at https://github.com/XRPLF/xrpl.js/blob/main/packages/xrpl/src/models/transactions/batch.ts before implementing
Create src/commands/batch.ts and register it in src/commands/index.ts and src/index.ts
xrpl batch with: --tx <json> (repeatable, from --build-only output), --txs-file <path> (JSON array alternative), --mode all-or-nothing|only-one|until-failure|independent (required)
Standard key material options: --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Standard output options: --node, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
CLI adds to each inner tx: tfInnerBatchTxn flag (bitwise OR into Flags), Fee: '0', SigningPubKey: '', removes TxnSignature/Signers/LastLedgerSequence
Outer Batch tx Flags from --mode: all-or-nothing=0x00010000, only-one=0x00020000, until-failure=0x00040000, independent=0x00080000
CLI exits with error if neither --tx nor --txs-file is provided
CLI exits with error if both --tx and --txs-file are provided
CLI exits with error if --mode is missing or invalid
CLI exits with error if any --tx value is not valid JSON
CLI exits with error if --txs-file does not exist or is not a valid JSON array
CLI exits with error if any inner tx has TransactionType 'Batch' (nested batch forbidden)
tests/e2e/batch/batch.validation.test.ts: no network, no beforeAll; no input, both inputs, invalid JSON, missing mode, invalid mode, nested batch — use hardcoded static values

tests/e2e/batch/batch.network.test.ts: beforeAll uses fundMaster + initTicketPool(client, master, 15); afterAll disconnects; all tests use it.concurrent; each it.concurrent calls createFunded(client, master, 2, 3) for fresh [sender, receiver]; tests: 2-tx batch using --tx flags (use sender --build-only payment + --build-only payment), --txs-file with JSON array, mode all-or-nothing (both succeed), mode independent, --json, --dry-run; budget: 15×0.2 + 10×3 = 33 ≤ 99
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Each it.concurrent in batch.network.test.ts creates its own sender+receiver pair. The --build-only output from inner commands is generated inline using runCLI with --build-only and the test's sender seed, then passed to xrpl batch via --tx. No shared wallets across tests.
